interactions.final_answer:
## PHASE 0 — DESIGN PROPOSAL (end-to-end intranet hub) ### 1) SCOPE CONFIG (IKEA) **Scope name:** IKEA Co-Worker Hub **Description:**

A home for co-worker information, policies, learning, and leadership updates across IKEA’s two-group structure—Ingka Group and Inter IKEA Group.

**Welcome message:**

Welcome, co-worker. Here you’ll find what’s happening across IKEA—what changed, what it means for you, and where to get help fast.

**Scope hero image:** Use brand logo (uploaded in Phase 1.5) from: - https://img.logo.dev/ikea.com?token=pk_OwST1E04QH-w3oM0YksM2Q&retina=true **Brand colors (from profile)** - primary_color: **#0058AB** / primary_color_contrast: **#FFFFFF** - secondary_color: **#FBD914** - warn_color: **#E00751** / warn_color_contrast: **#FFFFFF** **Highlights** - number_of_highlights: **4** - highlights_carousel: **true** - highlights_style: **BannerCarousel** **Footer (3 columns with real links from profile)** 1. Title: About IKEA - Link: https://about.ikea.com - Link: https://www.ikeafoundation.org 2. Title: News & Performance - Link: https://www.ingka.com/newsroom/ - Link: https://www.inter.ikea.com/en/newsroom/ - Link: https://www.ingka.com/annual-summary-and-sustainability-report/ 3. Title: Support & Careers - Link: https://www.ikea.com/global/en/this-is-ikea/work-with-us/ - Link: https://www.ikea.com/global/en/our-business/sustainability/ **Social links (global official handles from profile)** - LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/ikea/ - Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/ikea/ - YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/ikea - X/Twitter: https://twitter.com/IKEA **navigation_links** - topBarLinks (3): - https://www.ingka.com/newsroom/ - https://www.inter.ikea.com/en/newsroom/ - https://about.ikea.com - footerLinks (5): - https://www.ikea.com/global/en/our-business/sustainability/ - https://www.ikeafoundation.org - https://www.ingka.com/annual-summary-and-sustainability-report/ - https://www.inter.ikea.com/en/performance/ - https://www.ikea.com/global/en/this-is-ikea/work-with-us/ - socialLinks: as listed above **components (pick 1–3 based on profile)** - recentDocuments - upcomingEvents - workAnniversaries --- ### 2) CHANNEL ARCHITECTURE (5 channels) Legend: **PRIMARY = pinned & most visible** (3 channels). **SECONDARY = utility** (2 channels). #### Ch1 (PRIMARY) — “IKEA Inside Updates” - Purpose: Operational, factual intranet updates from across Ingka Group and Inter IKEA Group. - Type: public - Color: **#0058AB** - is_sticky: **True** - image_url (stock): **stock/Message.png** - FAQ: 1) Q: Where do I find the latest leadership notes? A: Start with “IKEA Inside Updates,” then look for the latest CEO / Group memo post. 2) Q: What do the headlines mean for my day-to-day work? A: Each update includes “What this changes for you” near the end. 3) Q: Are updates translated for co-workers? A: IKEA localises internal content into 11+ languages—English is the common business language, but the hub supports localisation. - hide_on_homepage_feed: False - hide_highlights: False - Description:

Quick reads that respect co-workers’ time: what changed, why it matters, and where to go next.

From new store formats to partnership pilots, this is where “the IKEA way” shows up in real actions.

- Tabs: ['Articles'] - initial_tab: 'Articles' - default_role: Reader #### Ch2 (PRIMARY) — “Democratic Design & Product Pipeline” - Purpose: Clear updates on how product decisions are made—cost-first, practical, and durable. - Type: public - Color: **#0071BC** (cool blue accent from profile) - is_sticky: **True** - image_url (stock): **stock/Innovation.png** - FAQ: 1) Q: What does “Democratic Design” require in practice? A: The five dimensions—Form, Function, Quality, Sustainability, and Low Price—must all be satisfied together. 2) Q: Why does affordability lead the design? A: Designers set the price target first, then engineer backwards so affordability is not a late compromise. 3) Q: How is sustainability tracked beyond slogans? A: IKEA reports progress and targets (e.g., renewable electricity matching progress) and treats responsibility as a daily decision. - hide_on_homepage_feed: False - hide_highlights: False - Description:

Democratic Design is the engine behind Better Everyday Life—price target first, then design around it.

Here you’ll find product pipeline context, circularity signals, and engineering decisions explained in plain language.

- Tabs: ['Articles'] - initial_tab: 'Articles' - default_role: Reader #### Ch3 (PRIMARY) — “People & Belonging (Tillsammans)” - Purpose: Co-worker programs, culture, and learning—Tack!, leadership development, and wellbeing rhythms. - Type: public - Color: **#FBD914** (warm tone accent) - is_sticky: **True** - image_url (stock): **stock/community.png** - FAQ: 1) Q: What is Tack! and who is it for? A: Tack! is a pension contribution programme for eligible co-workers, with solidarity across the organisation. 2) Q: What’s the standard full-time work week? A: IKEA uses a 36-hour standard full-time work week (not 40). 3) Q: How does “One IKEA Bonus” work? A: It’s based on unit-level performance—everyone in the same unit shares the same goal. - hide_on_homepage_feed: False - hide_highlights: False - Description:

Co-worker programmes that reinforce equality, togetherness, and growth—without corporate jargon.

You’ll find practical guides, recognition updates, and “what to do next” for learning and mobility.

- Tabs: ['Articles'] - initial_tab: 'Articles' - default_role: Reader #### Ch4 (SECONDARY) — “Policies, Security & Read Confirmation” - Purpose: Legal/security/compliance requirements where co-workers must acknowledge they’ve read. - Type: corporate - Color: **#E00751** - is_sticky: False - image_url (stock): **stock/security.png** - frequent_questions: (creation will require this field; content below will be in FAQ) 1) Q: Why do some items require “I have read this”? A: For policy, compliance, legal, and security topics IKEA needs explicit acknowledgement. 2) Q: What should I do if I spot a security concern? A: Follow your local security escalation path—log the concern and avoid sharing details in public channels. 3) Q: Where can I confirm the latest policy version? A: Each policy post includes the effective update context and points back to the most current hub entry. - hide_on_homepage_feed: True - hide_highlights: True - Description:

Clear, versioned policies that matter—built for acknowledgement, not scanning.

For security and compliance topics, this channel makes “what you must do next” unmistakable.

- Tabs: ['Articles', 'Pages'] - initial_tab: 'Articles' - default_role: None #### Ch5 (SECONDARY) — “Learning & Resources (On the Way)” - Purpose: Onboarding, training links, and reference material to help co-workers ramp up quickly. - Type: private - Color: **#111111** (neutral/dark) - is_sticky: False - image_url (stock): **stock/online_learning.png** - FAQ: 1) Q: How does onboarding usually combine classroom and on-the-job learning? A: IKEA onboarding is built from both on-site practice and structured learning blocks. 2) Q: Where can I find digital learning resources? A: Use this hub’s Learning section and the embedded training entry points. 3) Q: Can I learn through storytelling programmes like Let’s Co-Report? A: Yes—storytelling is an encouraged path for co-workers to share what they learn on the floor. - hide_on_homepage_feed: True - hide_highlights: True - Description:

Practical learning resources for new co-workers and people updating their skills.

Designed to keep you moving—short reads, clear next steps, and links to deeper learning.

- Tabs: ['Articles', 'Pages'] - initial_tab: 'Articles' - default_role: None --- ### 3) CONTENT CALENDAR (live, populated) **Highlights rule:** exactly **4** items total across the hub will have **is_main_highlight=True**. > Images in content: assigned ONLY from your stock list by closest topic match; if none, set null. #### Ch1: “IKEA Inside Updates” (PRIMARY) — 3 items 1) **Title:** “Prices down again: FY25 context and what it means for co-workers” - Type: announcement - tags: ['price-cuts', 'FY25', 'many-people', 'operations'] - **is_main_highlight: True** - is_sticky: True - require_read_confirmation: False - status: Published - notify: True - image_url: stock/financial_report.png - Body outline: Explain FY24 price reductions (€2.1B), FY25 continued lowering, revenue decline (-0.9% to €41.5B), profit recovery (+75% to €1.4B), and “what changes for you” in stores and support. CTA to check the policy/FAQ where needed. 2) **Title:** “Oxford Street in focus: what the 82,000 sq ft prototype teaches us” - Type: spotlight - tags: ['store-formats', 'UK', 'urban-retail', 'democratic-design'] - is_main_highlight: False - is_sticky: True - require_read_confirmation: False - status: Published - notify: False - image_url: stock/office.png - Body outline: Reference May 2025 opening, six floors, no car park, electric heating with heat pumps, Swedish deli/restaurant, “As-Is” section, and lessons for city formats. 3) **Title:** “Leadership handover, plain language: Juvencio Maeztu and Jakub Jankowski” - Type: recap - tags: ['leadership', 'CEO', 'Ingka', 'Inter IKEA'] - is_main_highlight: False - is_sticky: False - require_read_confirmation: False - status: Published - notify: False - image_url: stock/leadership_speech.png - Body outline: Mention November 2025 (Maeztu), January 2026 (Jankowski), succession details, and the mandate for faster commercial urgency while staying true to culture. #### Ch2: “Democratic Design & Product Pipeline” (PRIMARY) — 3 items 1) **Title:** “Democratic Design, measured: Form, Function, Quality, Sustainability, Low Price” - Type: guide - tags: ['democratic-design', 'quality', 'sustainability', 'low-price'] - **is_main_highlight: True** - is_sticky: True - require_read_confirmation: False - status: Published - notify: False - image_url: stock/QualityCheck.png - Body outline: Explain the five dimensions, why affordability must be met, price-first engineering, and how co-workers can use the checklist in everyday decisions. 2) **Title:** “Circularity at scale: IKEA second-hand marketplace expands from Sweden” - Type: milestone - tags: ['circularity', 'second-hand', 'Sweden', 'as-is'] - is_main_highlight: False - is_sticky: False - require_read_confirmation: False - status: Published - notify: True - image_url: stock/sustainability.png - Body outline: Reference Jan 2026 launch in Sweden and planned expansion to Spain, Norway, Portugal, Poland; connect to “As-Is” sections. 3) **Title:** “Renewable electricity progress: FY25 94.8% matched—what’s next toward 2030” - Type: recap - tags: ['renewables', 'FY25', 'targets', 'energy'] - is_main_highlight: False - is_sticky: False - require_read_confirmation: False - status: Published - notify: False - image_url: stock/DataMaintenance.png - Body outline: Mention goal of 100% by 2025, actual 94.8%, €4.2B invested/committed and €3.3B planned through 2030; keep it practical. #### Ch3: “People & Belonging (Tillsammans)” (PRIMARY) — 3 items 1) **Title:** “36 hours that feel like more: how the work-week supports a Better Everyday Life” - Type: guide - tags: ['work-week', 'wellbeing', 'tillsammans', 'benefits'] - **is_main_highlight: True** - is_sticky: True - require_read_confirmation: False - status: Published - notify: False - image_url: stock/community.png - Body outline: State 36-hour standard week, contrast with typical 40, describe practical benefits, and link to loyalty leave details. 2) **Title:** “Loyalty leave explained: two days after five years—and more after each block” - Type: guide - tags: ['loyalty-leave', 'benefits', 'time-off', 'tenure'] - is_main_highlight: False - is_sticky: False - require_read_confirmation: False - status: Published - notify: False - image_url: stock/Checklist.png - Body outline: Explain two days after five years, one added day for every subsequent five-year block, and connect to retirement bonus after 10+ years. 3) **Title:** “Tack! pensions and One IKEA Bonus: solidarity by design” - Type: spotlight - tags: ['tack', 'pensions', 'bonus', 'culture'] - is_main_highlight: False - is_sticky: False - require_read_confirmation: False - status: Published - notify: False - image_url: stock/leadership_speech.png - Body outline: Describe Tack! universal contribution amount and One IKEA Bonus unit-level goals; close with a “talk to your local HR/People & Culture contact” style CTA (without fabricating names). #### Ch4: “Policies, Security & Read Confirmation” (CORPORATE) — 2 items 1) **Title:** “Policy acknowledgement required: Security basics for co-workers (what to do today)” - Type: policy - tags: ['security', 'policy', 'acknowledgement', 'safe-work'] - is_main_highlight: False - is_sticky: True - require_read_confirmation: **True** - status: Published - notify: True - image_url: stock/security.png - Body outline: Provide practical steps (password hygiene, safe handling, reporting concern) aligned with “Password” & “Security” concepts; end with acknowledgement CTA. 2) **Title:** “Compliance update: Record-keeping and document review expectations” - Type: policy - tags: ['compliance', 'document-review', 'audit', 'acknowledgement'] - is_main_highlight: False - is_sticky: False - require_read_confirmation: **True** - status: Published - notify: False - image_url: stock/document_review.png - Body outline: Explain why record-keeping matters, how “audit” supports improvement, and instruct co-workers to confirm they’ve read and follow the current process. #### Ch5: “Learning & Resources (On the Way)” (PRIVATE) — 2 items + 1 event total in hub 1) **Title:** “New co-worker onboarding: the first 30 days, in the IKEA way” - Type: guide - tags: ['onboarding', 'learning', 'first-30-days', 'on-the-job'] - is_main_highlight: False - is_sticky: False - require_read_confirmation: False - status: Published - notify: False - image_url: stock/onboarding.png - Body outline: Describe hybrid model (on-site + classroom), how to find resources here, and encourage using co-worker storytelling (Let’s Co-Report) early. 2) **Event Title:** “Town hall: Questions from co-workers, answered live” - Type: event - tags: ['town-hall', 'leadership', 'Q&A', 'tillsammans'] - is_main_highlight: False - is_sticky: False - require_read_confirmation: False - status: Published - notify: True - image_url: stock/video_call.png - **event_start:** 2026-06-18T15:00:00Z - **event_end:** 2026-06-18T16:00:00Z - location: IKEA global virtual town hall - Body outline: How the session works (open questions, no pre-screening), and what to bring (your practical “what does this change for me?” questions). **Events total across hub:** 1 (kept to match “1–2 events total” and avoid overfilling). --- ### 4) HUB-LEVEL POLISH (hub-wide settings) - company_name: IKEA - website_name: IKEA Co-Worker Hub - default_language: en-US (infer from English operating model; if you prefer EU-first, tell me) - supported_languages: infer from “11+ languages” but you only provided “English is common business language”; so I will set: ['en-US'] unless you give specific regions. - login_quick_links (5) from profile: - https://www.ikea.com/global/en/this-is-ikea/work-with-us/ - https://www.ingka.com/newsroom/ - https://www.inter.ikea.com/en/newsroom/ - https://about.ikea.com - https://www.ikeafoundation.org - sidebar_navigation (ordered): 1) collaborators 2) documents 3) learning 4) workgroups 5) approvalRequests (Reason: strong people/culture + policy acknowledgement + learning; ideation/apps not mentioned in the profile beyond learning & storytelling.) --- ## Confirmation required This is the complete design. **Shall I build it end-to-end**, or would you like to adjust anything first — **channel names, content plan, colors, or hub settings**?

arguments.name: Ikea Inside
arguments.description: A home for co-worker information, policies, learning, and leadership updates across IKEA’s two-group structure—Ingka Group and Inter IKEA Group.
arguments.welcome_message: Welcome, co-worker. Here you’ll find what’s happening across IKEA—what changed, what it means for you, and where to get help fast.

channels.name: IKEA Inside Updates
channels.description:
Quick reads that respect co-workers’ time: what changed, why it matters, and where to go next.

From new store formats to partnership pilots, this is where “the IKEA way” shows up in real actions.

frequent_questions.question: Where do I find the latest leadership notes?
frequent_questions.answer: Start with “IKEA Inside Updates,” then look for the latest CEO / Group memo post.

frequent_questions.question: What do the headlines mean for my day-to-day work?
frequent_questions.answer: Each update includes “What this changes for you” near the end.

frequent_questions.question: Are updates translated for co-workers?
frequent_questions.answer: IKEA localises internal content into 11+ languages—English is the common business language, but the hub supports localisation.

channels.name: Democratic Design & Product Pipeline
channels.description:
Democratic Design is the engine behind Better Everyday Life—price target first, then design around it.

Here you’ll find product pipeline context, circularity signals, and engineering decisions explained in plain language.

frequent_questions.question: What does “Democratic Design” require in practice?
frequent_questions.answer: The five dimensions—Form, Function, Quality, Sustainability, and Low Price—must all be satisfied together.

frequent_questions.question: Why does affordability lead the design?
frequent_questions.answer: Designers set the price target first, then engineer backwards so affordability is not a late compromise.

frequent_questions.question: How is sustainability tracked beyond slogans?
frequent_questions.answer: IKEA reports progress and targets (e.g., renewable electricity matching progress) and treats responsibility as a daily decision.

channels.name: People & Belonging (Tillsammans)
channels.description:
Co-worker programmes that reinforce equality, togetherness, and growth—without corporate jargon.

You’ll find practical guides, recognition updates, and “what to do next” for learning and mobility.

frequent_questions.question: What is Tack! and who is it for?
frequent_questions.answer: Tack! is a pension contribution programme for eligible co-workers, with solidarity across the organisation.

frequent_questions.question: What’s the standard full-time work week?
frequent_questions.answer: IKEA uses a 36-hour standard full-time work week (not 40).

frequent_questions.question: How does “One IKEA Bonus” work?
frequent_questions.answer: It’s based on unit-level performance—everyone in the same unit shares the same goal.

channels.name: Policies, Security & Read Confirmation
channels.description:
Clear, versioned policies that matter—built for acknowledgement, not scanning.

For security and compliance topics, this channel makes “what you must do next” unmistakable.

frequent_questions.question: Why do some items require “I have read this”?
frequent_questions.answer: For policy, compliance, legal, and security topics IKEA needs explicit acknowledgement.

frequent_questions.question: What should I do if I spot a security concern?
frequent_questions.answer: Follow your local security escalation path—log the concern and avoid sharing details in public channels.

frequent_questions.question: Where can I confirm the latest policy version?
frequent_questions.answer: Each policy post includes the effective update context and points back to the most current hub entry.

channels.name: Learning & Resources (On the Way)
channels.description:
Practical learning resources for new co-workers and people updating their skills.

Designed to keep you moving—short reads, clear next steps, and links to deeper learning.

frequent_questions.question: How does onboarding usually combine classroom and on-the-job learning?
frequent_questions.answer: IKEA onboarding is built from both on-site practice and structured learning blocks.

frequent_questions.question: Where can I find digital learning resources?
frequent_questions.answer: Use this hub’s Learning section and the embedded training entry points.

frequent_questions.question: Can I learn through storytelling programmes like Let’s Co-Report?
frequent_questions.answer: Yes—storytelling is an encouraged path for co-workers to share what they learn on the floor.
